theme: Every static element is a sleeping story — make the monitors glow, the sync narrate, and the whiteboard advise

ideas.title: Coffee Corner Conversation Cluster
ideas.description: The coffee corner is purely decorative — an emoji and a label. But idle workers already have wander tooltips saying 'Heading to the coffee corner', implying social dynamics that the corner itself never acknowledges. When idleWorkers.length >= 2, render a small badge on the coffee-corner element: a speech-bubble cluster SVG (two overlapping rounded rects, 18×10px each, slight rotation) with a '☕ chatting' label. When idleWorkers.length drops to 0 or 1, the badge fades out. No new data or state — idleWorkers is already computed in Office.jsx. The badge sits above the coffee-corner div, z-index above the floor, with a 0.3s fade-in/out CSS transition. Add a faint radial glow around the corner element during the clustered state using a CSS filter. WHY: The wandering system already tells the story of workers drifting toward the coffee machine — the corner itself staying mute breaks the narrative. A crowd badge makes the coffee corner the office's de facto social dashboard: you can tell at a glance whether it's a heads-down sprint day or a chatty, low-energy afternoon, without reading a single number.

ideas.title: Vibe History Sparkline in StatsBar
ideas.description: The StatsBar shows the current vibe as a label but has no memory. On every vibe change (detected in the vibeStreak useEffect in App.jsx where previousVibeKeyRef.current updates), append {key, ts: Date.now()} to a localStorage array under 'bloberto-vibe-history', capped at 12 entries. In the StatsBar vibe card, render a 64×14px inline SVG sparkline to the right of the vibe label: each entry is a filled circle (r=3) color-coded by key (crushing=#a78bfa, in-flow=#2dd4bf, on-fire=#ef4444, slow-day=#9ca3af, after-hours=#6366f1), connected by a thin polyline (stroke: var(--text-muted), width 0.8). The circles are evenly spaced horizontally. Wrap each circle in a <title> for hover tooltip: vibe label + relative timestamp. Read from localStorage on mount; if empty, seed with the current vibe. WHY: Vibe is currently a scalar — you see only now. The sparkline makes it a story arc. You can see in 2 seconds whether the team has been stuck in on-fire all morning or whether they have been cycling. It converts passive monitoring into temporal awareness with zero new fetches and about 30 lines of code.

ideas.title: Desk Monitor Active Glow
ideas.description: The desk monitors (desk__monitor) are static gray rectangles — the same visual whether the desk is vacant, occupied-working, or occupied-idle. The desk element already has --role-color set as a CSS custom property when a working worker occupies it (Office.jsx line 703). Add a desk__monitor--active class when the occupant is working, with a CSS @keyframes monitor-glow animation: 0%,100% { box-shadow: 0 0 3px var(--role-color, #6b7280); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 10px var(--role-color, #6b7280); } — 3s ease-in-out infinite. Add desk__monitor--idle for idle occupants: same animation in #9ca3af at 40% opacity, 6s cycle. Vacant desks get a dim slowly-drifting white screensaver dot via a translate keyframe. Also add a 2px horizontal scan-line pseudo-element on active monitors that drifts top-to-bottom on a 4s loop. WHY: Monitors are the single most natural readout of desk engagement. Right now a working desk and a vacant desk look nearly identical at floor zoom level. Glowing role-colored monitors make the floor legible at a glance — you can read the team energy state without parsing name labels or visiting the Dashboard.

ideas.title: Sync-Reactive Bloberto Narration
ideas.description: Bloberto shows typing dots while isSyncing is true, but the moment sync resolves he goes silent — a missed storytelling beat. After each syncFromGitHub() call resolves, derive a syncResultKey from the outcome: 'new-arrivals' (appeared.length > 0), 'errors' (errorCount > 0 and was 0 before), 'vibe-up' (vibeRanking increased), 'vibe-down', or 'steady'. Pass this as a new prop syncResultKey to Office then to Bloberto's Character. In the Character useEffect that watches managerVibe, also watch syncResultKey: when it changes to a non-null value, immediately fire a priority speech bubble with a curated one-liner — 'oh good, reinforcements 📥' for new-arrivals, 'who broke it this time 🫠' for errors, 'told you we would turn it around 💅' for vibe-up, 'we had a good run' for vibe-down, 'carrying on as planned' for steady. This quote gets a 5s display window and preempts the ambient 45s loop. Reset syncResultKey to null after the quote fires. WHY: Every 30-second poll is a narrative beat that currently happens invisibly. Bloberto as a live post-sync narrator converts a background fetch into a tiny story moment — he stops being a decorative blob and becomes an actual manager who notices when things change.

ideas.title: Error Worker Office Ticker
ideas.description: When workers are in error state the office floor currently shows only a glitch animation and a red ! badge on the avatar — easy to miss at a glance. Add an ErrorTicker component in Office.jsx that receives errorWorkers (workers filtered to status === 'error'). When errorWorkers.length > 0, render a fixed strip at the very bottom of the office-floor div above the door: height 18px, background rgba(239,68,68,0.12), border-top: 1px solid #ef444455. The inner content is a wide div (width: fit-content, min-width: 200%) containing concatenated entries: '⚠️ Carlos: Deploy pipeline failed · ⚠️ Maya: Component crash · ' (task text truncated at 38 chars, content duplicated for seamless loop). This inner div animates with @keyframes ticker-scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } over 18s linear infinite. When errorWorkers.length drops to zero, the strip fades out over 0.4s and unmounts. On initial appearance it badge-pops in from the bottom. WHY: Errors are the most urgent office state but the floor is where most eyes are. The ticker broadcasts the crisis as ambient background information — operators watching the floor passively get alerted without needing to open the Dashboard. It makes the office floor a real monitoring surface.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Rotating Advice Lines
ideas.description: The office whiteboard renders a single static string all day (VIBE_WHITEBOARD[vibe]). Add a second rotating advisory line below the vibe label — a VIBE_WHITEBOARD_TIPS export in constants.js with 3-4 short strings per vibe. Examples: crushing: ['Merge it.', 'Deploy window: NOW', 'Sprint velocity: ∞'], on-fire: ['Stop. Breathe. Rollback?', 'Who has prod access?', 'ETA on the fix?'], in-flow: ['No meetings til 4pm.', 'PRs need review.', 'Hydrate.'], slow-day: ['Backlog grooming?', 'One ticket. One focus.', 'Write the docs.'], after-hours: ['Go home.', 'The deploy can wait.', 'This is a commit crime.']. In Office.jsx maintain a tipIndex state that increments every 20s via setInterval (reset to 0 on vibe change). The tip renders in a smaller font below the main label with a CSS fade+slide-up keyframe transition between rotations (opacity 0→1, translateY 4px→0, 0.4s ease). WHY: The whiteboard is the highest-attention fixed element on the floor and it currently says the same thing all day. Rotating micro-advice transforms it into a living team pulse display — something worth glancing at every few minutes, like a real shared whiteboard that someone keeps updating.

ideas.title: Progress Ring Milestone Burst on Floor
ideas.description: Worker cards on the Dashboard already fire a flash animation at progress milestones (25/50/75/100%) via the flashClass logic in ProgressBar. But the office floor progress ring around active worker avatars is visually silent even when someone just crossed 50% or finished. In the Character component (Office.jsx), add prevProgressRef = useRef(worker.progress). On each render where worker.progress !== prevProgressRef.current, compare against milestones [50, 100]. At 50% crossing: fire a CSS class ring-burst on the progress-ring SVG — @keyframes ring-burst { 0% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.6; transform: scale(1.3); } 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } } 0.5s ease-out. At 100%: ring-burst plus briefly overlay a centered checkmark text element inside the SVG (font-size 10px, fill #22c55e, fade out over 1.2s). The burst state is a boolean useState, cleared after the animation duration via setTimeout. WHY: The floor is where most observers spend their time, but it is currently disconnected from work progress data beyond a static ring fill. Milestone bursts make progress legible from the floor — you see someone just shipped without opening the Dashboard. It closes the gap between the floor as decoration and the floor as monitoring surface.
